pairs.economic_rationale: Two bulge-bracket investment banks with similar revenue mix (trading, advisory, wealth mgmt). Same regulatory regime (Fed/OCC), correlated M&A and IPO cycle exposure.
Precious metals ETFs. Gold and silver are historically correlated via safe-haven demand and real rate sensitivity. Silver has higher beta to gold.
Large-cap money-center banks. Similar interest rate sensitivity, loan book composition, and regulatory capital requirements.
Integrated oil majors. Both upstream/downstream, similar geographic exposure, correlated via crude oil prices and refining margins.
x86 CPU competitors. Market share shifts between them, correlated via PC/server demand cycles and semiconductor capex.
Large-format retailers competing for consumer wallet share. Similar traffic patterns, correlated via consumer spending and inflation expectations.
Home improvement duopoly. Nearly identical business models, correlated via housing market activity, renovation cycles, and lumber prices.
Payment network duopoly. Both earn interchange fees on consumer spending. Near-identical business models, correlated via cross-border travel and e-commerce growth.
Ride-sharing competitors in overlapping US markets. Similar driver economics, regulatory exposure, and demand sensitivity to gas prices.
Streaming competitors. Both invest heavily in content, competing for subscriber attention. Correlated via advertising market and consumer entertainment spend.
Package delivery duopoly. Similar network economics, correlated via e-commerce volumes, fuel costs, and labor market conditions.
Major US airlines with similar hub-and-spoke networks. Correlated via jet fuel prices, travel demand, and pilot labor costs.
Consumer-focused money-center banks. Similar loan portfolios (mortgages, credit cards), correlated via interest rates and credit cycle.
Semiconductor companies (memory vs analog). Both cyclical, correlated via semiconductor capex cycle and end-market demand.
E&P focused oil companies. Both primarily upstream, correlated via crude oil prices and US shale production economics.
Financial sector ETF and its largest component. Should be REJECTED by ETF-component filter.
Semiconductor ETF and its largest holding. Should be REJECTED by ETF-component filter.
Telecom duopoly. Similar subscriber economics, 5G capex cycles, and dividend profiles. Correlated via wireless subscriber growth and spectrum costs.
Refiners with similar crack spread exposure. Both primarily downstream, correlated via refining margins and crude-product spreads.
Quick-service restaurant leaders. Both franchise-heavy models, correlated via consumer traffic, food costs, and labor inflation.
